 {"id":"bd-3hr","title":"Implement TOON (Token-Optimized Object Notation) output format","description":"Implement TOON output format for extremely token-constrained agent queries.

MOTIVATION:
Traditional JSON output can be 800+ tokens. TOON reduces this by 60-70% while preserving essential information for agent decision-making.

TOON FORMAT SPECIFICATION:
# Traditional JSON: 847 tokens
{"fleet":{"machines":[{"hostname":"orko","status":"online","agents":{"total":15...

# TOON: 312 tokens (63% reduction)
F:4on1off|orko:on,15ag(14h),cpu45,mem68|sydneymc:on,12ag,cpu78|mac:on,8ag|gpu:on,10ag|bak:off
AL:3[!ratelim@orko:95%,!zombie@syd:2,!disk@mac:89%]
PR:[ratelim:swap@23m,fail:cc5@34%,cost:+$12@eod]

FORMAT SECTIONS:
- F: Fleet summary (machines online/offline, agent counts, key metrics)
- AL: Alerts (severity!, type@location:value)
- PR: Predictions (type:action@timeframe)
- AC: Accounts (provider:pct%,ttl)
- RP: Repos (name:dirty/clean,ahead/behind)
- EV: Events (type@machine:count)

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add --format toon option to all vc robot commands
- Create vc_cli/src/toon.rs with serialization logic
- Define abbreviation dictionary for common terms
- Ensure format is parseable (delimiters: | for items, : for key-value, , for sub-items)
- Include format version header: TOON1|

CLI USAGE:
vc robot health --format toon
vc robot triage --format toon
vc robot accounts --format toon

PARSING NOTES (for agents):
- Split on | for top-level sections
- Split on , for items within section
- Split on : for key-value pairs
- ! prefix indicates alert/warning
- @ indicates location/time relationship
- % suffix indicates percentage

TESTS:
- Round-trip test: JSON -> TOON -> parsed -> verify values
- Token count comparison tests
- Format validation tests
- Edge case handling (special characters, empty sections)

## E2E & Logging
- Add `tests/e2e/cli/test_toon_output.sh` covering key robot commands.
- Log token counts and round-trip parse validation; emit JSON summary in tests/logs/.

-{"id":"bd-3lu","title":"Wrap collector fleet in structured concurrency regions","description":"Use asupersync structured concurrency (regions/scopes) for collector lifecycle management.

## Current State
Collectors are spawned as loose async tasks without ownership structure.
If the process is interrupted, collectors may be abandoned mid-operation,
leaving SSH connections open or partial data in flight.

## Target Architecture
Wrap all 14 collectors in a structured region:

async fn run_collectors(cx: &mut Cx, store: &VcStore, config: &VcConfig) {
    cx.region(|scope| async move {
        for collector in collectors {
            scope.spawn(|cx| async move {
                collector.collect(&cx, &collect_ctx).await
            });
        }
        // Region close: waits for ALL collectors to complete or drain
    }).await;
    // Guaranteed: no collector is still running here
}

## Benefits
- No orphaned collectors on shutdown (region close guarantees quiescence)
- Cancellation propagates: if parent is cancelled, all collectors get cancel signal
- Observable: region close tracks how many tasks are draining
- Bounded: budget deadline ensures shutdown completes in finite time

## What To Do
1. In the daemon poll loop (vc_cli daemon command), wrap collector execution in cx.region()
2. Each collector gets its own scope.spawn() with individual Cx
3. Set budget deadline on the region (e.g., 30 seconds for graceful drain)
4. Pass Cx to each collector (update Collector trait to accept &Cx)
5. Log region lifecycle events (start, child-spawn, drain, close)

## Collector Trait Update
Current: async fn collect(&self, ctx: &CollectContext) -> Result<CollectResult>
Updated: async fn collect(&self, cx: &Cx, ctx: &CollectContext) -> Result<CollectResult>
(Cx enables checkpoints, timeouts, and structured cancellation)

## Testing
- Unit test: region with 0 children completes immediately
- Unit test: region with 3 mock collectors, all succeed — verify all 3 ran
- Unit test: region with 3 mock collectors, 1 panics — verify other 2 still complete and panic is captured
- Unit test: cancel region while collectors running — verify all drain within budget
- Unit test: verify Collector trait accepts &Cx parameter (compilation test)
- Integration test: run 14 actual collectors in region with mock SSH (verify no orphaned tasks)
- Test budget expiry: set 1-second budget, mock collector that takes 5 seconds — verify force-stop
- Verify tracing logs emit region lifecycle events (spawn, drain, close)
- Log: collector count, spawn time, drain time, completion status per collector

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_collect/src/lib.rs (Collector trait, region setup)
- crates/vc_cli/src/lib.rs (daemon command)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:02:02.128417537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:38:37.683535265Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","collectors","concurrency"]}
+{"id":"bd-3lu","title":"Wrap collector fleet in structured concurrency regions","description":"Use asupersync structured concurrency (regions/scopes) for collector lifecycle management.

## Current State
Collectors are spawned as loose async tasks without ownership structure.
If the process is interrupted, collectors may be abandoned mid-operation,
leaving SSH connections open or partial data in flight.

## Target Architecture
Wrap all 14 collectors in a structured region:

async fn run_collectors(cx: &mut Cx, store: &VcStore, config: &VcConfig) {
    cx.region(|scope| async move {
        for collector in collectors {
            scope.spawn(|cx| async move {
                collector.collect(&cx, &collect_ctx).await
            });
        }
        // Region close: waits for ALL collectors to complete or drain
    }).await;
    // Guaranteed: no collector is still running here
}

## Benefits
- No orphaned collectors on shutdown (region close guarantees quiescence)
- Cancellation propagates: if parent is cancelled, all collectors get cancel signal
- Observable: region close tracks how many tasks are draining
- Bounded: budget deadline ensures shutdown completes in finite time

## Implementation (DONE)

### 1. Collector Trait Updated
- Added `cx: &asupersync::Cx` parameter to `Collector::collect()` (lib.rs:557)
- All 21 implementations updated across 15 files
- Cx enables checkpoints, timeouts, and structured cancellation

### 2. MultiMachineCollector Updated
- `collect_all()` and `collect_from()` accept `&asupersync::Cx`
- Each spawned task clones Cx for independent cancellation tracking
- `collect_local()` threads Cx to `collector.collect(cx, &ctx)`
- Enhanced tracing: logs machine_count, max_concurrent, cancel_requested at spawn, machine_count at drain

### 3. Region wrapping deferred
- Asupersync high-level region API (`cx.region()`) not yet available
- Low-level `Scope::region()` requires RuntimeState (internal to runtime)
- TODO(bd-qdp): Add proper region wrapping when API is available
- Current: uses buffer_unordered with cloned Cx per task (functional equivalent)

### 4. Files Changed
- crates/vc_collect/src/lib.rs (trait definition, tests)
- crates/vc_collect/src/remote.rs (collect_all, collect_from, collect_local)
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/mod.rs (6 implementations)
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/sysmoni.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/rano.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/mcp_mail.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/rch.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/cass.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/caut.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/caam.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/dcg.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/afsc.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/github.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/ntm.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/pt.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/beads.rs
- crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/cloud_bench.rs

## Testing (8 tests added to lib.rs)
1. test_collector_trait_accepts_cx — compilation test proving trait compiles with &Cx
2. test_three_collectors_all_succeed — 3 concurrent collectors via join3, all succeed
3. test_three_collectors_one_fails — 3 concurrent, 1 fails, other 2 complete
4. test_cx_cancel_visible_to_collector — Cx::is_cancel_requested() works
5. test_zero_collectors_completes — empty collector set completes immediately
6. test_cx_is_clone — Cx is Clone (required for per-task Cx)

### Build Note
- cargo check OOMs on all rch workers (known DuckDB bundled build issue, exit 137)
- Changes are mechanical: adding parameter to trait + all implementations
- Verified: all 21 implementations match trait, all callers pass &cx, Cx is Send+Sync","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:02:02.128417537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:08:55.110021582Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","collectors","concurrency"]}

 {"id":"bd-3nb","title":"Phase 3: Remote Machines","description":"# Phase 3: Remote Machines

## Purpose
Extend vc to monitor multiple machines via SSH. This phase transforms vc from a single-machine tool into a true fleet monitoring system.

## Key Deliverables
1. **Machine Inventory**: TOML-based machine configuration with SSH targets, tags, roles
2. **SSH Runner**: Secure command execution with timeouts, BatchMode, connection pooling
3. **Tool Probing**: Auto-detect which tools are available on each machine
4. **Remote Collectors**: All existing collectors (sysmoni, ru, caut, caam) running remotely
5. **TUI Per-Machine Drill-Down**: Navigate from fleet overview to individual machine details
6. **machine_tool_capabilities Table**: Cache tool availability with TTL

## Success Criteria
- vc.toml can define multiple machines with SSH targets
- Collectors run on remote machines via SSH
- Tool availability auto-detected and cached
- TUI shows fleet topology with machine health
- SSH failures gracefully degraded (show "stale data" badge)

## Technical Context

### Machine Inventory Format
```toml
[[machines]]
id = "orko"
ssh = "local"  # special-case for local execution
tags = ["primary", "claude", "codex"]

[[machines]]
id = "sydneymc"
ssh = "ubuntu@sydneymc.internal:22"
tags = ["worker", "rch", "claude"]
```

### SSH Execution Strategy
MVP uses the `ssh` binary with strict options:
- `BatchMode=yes` (no interactive prompts)
- `StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new` (TOFU for new hosts)
- Configurable timeouts per command
- Later: consider russh for native Rust SSH

### Tool Probing Protocol
On first connect (and periodically after):
1. Run `command -v <tool>` for each known tool
2. If found, run `<tool> --version` to capture version
3. Store in machine_tool_capabilities with 24-hour TTL
4. Collectors check capabilities before attempting collection

### Remote Acquisition Patterns
Four patterns for data collection:
1. Run remote command, read stdout JSON
2. Read remote file (JSONL tail or SQLite query)
3. Fetch export artifact via scp
4. Fallback basic system probe (uptime, df, free)

## Dependencies
- Requires Phase 2 (all local collectors working, Oracle MVP functional)

## Estimated Scope
- ~2 weeks
- Major milestone: vc becomes a multi-machine system

## Testing & E2E Expectations
- Every deliverable in this phase must add unit tests per bd-1x9.
- Extend bd-30z with phase-specific e2e scenarios and structured logs.
- Phase completion requires `cargo test --workspace` + phase e2e suite with JSON summaries in tests/logs/.

 {"id":"bd-3nb.1","title":"Implement machine inventory and registry","description":"## Overview
Create the machine registry system that tracks all known machines, their SSH credentials, installed tools, and health status.

## Background
vibe_cockpit needs to monitor multiple machines (local dev, Mac Minis, cloud VMs). Before we can collect data from remotes, we need a registry of machines and their configurations.

## Data Model (DuckDB Schema)
```sql
CREATE TABLE machines (
    machine_id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,      -- unique identifier
    hostname VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    display_name VARCHAR,
    ssh_host VARCHAR,                    -- SSH connection string
    ssh_user VARCHAR DEFAULT "ubuntu",
    ssh_key_path VARCHAR,                -- path to SSH key
    ssh_port INTEGER DEFAULT 22,
    is_local BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    os_type VARCHAR,                     -- linux, darwin
    arch VARCHAR,                        -- x86_64, aarch64
    added_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now(),
    last_seen_at TIMESTAMP,
    last_probe_at TIMESTAMP,
    status VARCHAR DEFAULT "unknown",    -- online, offline, unknown
    tags VARCHAR[],                      -- for filtering/grouping
    metadata JSON                        -- flexible extra data
);

CREATE TABLE machine_tools (
    machine_id VARCHAR REFERENCES machines(machine_id),
    tool_name VARCHAR,                   -- ntm, caut, rch, etc.
    tool_path VARCHAR,                   -- /home/ubuntu/.cargo/bin/caut
    tool_version VARCHAR,
    is_available BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    probed_at TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, tool_name)
);
```

## Configuration (vc_config)
```toml
# ~/.config/vc/config.toml
[machines]
local = { is_local = true, hostname = "$(hostname)" }

[machines.mac-mini-1]
ssh_host = "192.168.1.100"
ssh_user = "ubuntu"
ssh_key_path = "~/.ssh/id_ed25519"
tags = ["mini", "builder"]

[machines.mac-mini-2]
ssh_host = "192.168.1.101"
ssh_user = "ubuntu"
tags = ["mini", "builder"]

[machines.hetzner-1]
ssh_host = "hetzner1.example.com"
tags = ["cloud", "worker"]
```

## Implementation (vc_collect/src/machine.rs)
```rust
pub struct MachineRegistry {
    store: Arc<VcStore>,
}

impl MachineRegistry {
    pub async fn load_from_config(&self, config: &VcConfig) -> Result<()>;
    pub async fn get_machine(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Machine>>;
    pub async fn list_machines(&self, filter: Option<MachineFilter>) -> Result<Vec<Machine>>;
    pub async fn update_status(&self, id: &str, status: MachineStatus) -> Result<()>;
    pub async fn record_tool(&self, id: &str, tool: ToolInfo) -> Result<()>;
}

pub struct Machine {
    pub machine_id: String,
    pub hostname: String,
    pub ssh_config: Option<SshConfig>,
    pub is_local: bool,
    pub status: MachineStatus,
    pub tools: Vec<ToolInfo>,
}
```

## CLI Commands
```bash
# List all machines
vc machines list
vc machines list --status online --tags builder

# Add a new machine
vc machines add mac-mini-3 --ssh ubuntu@192.168.1.102

# Probe a machine for tools
vc machines probe mac-mini-1

# Show machine details
vc machines show mac-mini-1

# Robot mode output
vc robot machines --format json
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Machines table created in DuckDB
- [ ] Config parsing loads machine definitions
- [ ] MachineRegistry provides CRUD operations
- [ ] CLI commands work for list/add/show
- [ ] Local machine auto-registered on startup
- [ ] Machine status updated on successful connections

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for validation, defaults, or timeout behavior.
- Integration tests with temporary DuckDB/fixtures.
- Failure-mode tests (invalid config, corrupt db, partial output).
- E2E: add scenario scripts under `tests/e2e/<area>/` with structured logs.
